cards.back: A data structure consisting of a collection of nodes, each containing a value and a reference (or link) to the next node in the sequence.
A type of linked list where each node only points to the next node, not to the previous one.
A data structure that contains references to itself, allowing it to traverse its own nodes or data.

cards.front: Insertion in a Singly Linked List
cards.back: To insert a new node at a specific position in a singly linked list, we need to update the 'next' pointers of adjacent nodes.
To delete a node from a linked list, we need to update the 'next' pointer of the previous node to skip the deleted node.
